summary: Soundboard recording with minor dropouts captures an energetic 1972 show highlighted by a driving Play In The Band, a smoky Dark Star>China Cat Sunflower>I Know You Rider, and standout performances of Jack Straw, Friend Of The Devil and Sugar Magnolia.
review: The two fan reviews are enthusiastic about the performance, noting urgent jams, creative set‑list choices and vocal fireworks from both Weir and Phil.  The soundboard source is generally clear but suffers a few audible drop‑outs and overly high vocal levels on some tracks.  Overall the show is regarded as a strong example of late‑1972 Dead magic.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is rated high due to multiple standout songs and energetic playing; recording quality is good but marred by minor drop‑outs, yielding a weighted average around 4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Minor but noticeable drop‑outs on the Jed & Playin' reprise and at the end of I Know You Rider; vocals (especially Weir) are mixed very high, but overall clarity is solid.

standout_songs: Dark Star > China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Creative set‑list with urgent jams, vocal blitzes and a strong second‑half flow; the band keeps high energy throughout.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil's vocals are described as over the top and prominently featured, earning praise for taking more lead.
